name: African Union
climate: Equatorial rainforest, savanna, Saharan desert, Mediterranean in the north, temperate in the south, highland in East Africa

neighbors.name: European Union
neighbors.relevance: Largest trade partner and development aid source for Africa; EU-Africa summits set trade agendas

neighbors.name: BRICS
neighbors.relevance: China and India are top trade partners; BRICS expansion includes African members (Egypt, Ethiopia)

neighbors.name: Gulf Cooperation Council
neighbors.relevance: Major investment flows, labor migration, and energy partnerships across North and East Africa

neighbors.name: Mediterranean Sea
neighbors.relevance: Northern African members border the Mediterranean; key migration and trade corridor to Europe

neighbors.name: Red Sea
neighbors.relevance: East African coast and Suez Canal access critical for continental trade routes

neighbors.name: Belt and Road Initiative
neighbors.relevance: Chinese infrastructure investment across the continent; ports, railways, and special economic zones

links.description: ECOWAS members are a subset of AU; West African integration feeds into continental AfCFTA
EAC customs union operates within AU framework; East African integration pillar
SADC members are AU members; southern African economic integration bloc
Sahel security zone spans multiple AU member states; conflict and counterterrorism coordination
Horn of Africa sub-region; strategic Red Sea access and ongoing conflict zones
Nigeria, Algeria, Libya, Angola, Congo are OPEC members; oil policy coordination
Egypt controls the Suez Canal; critical chokepoint for Africa-Asia-Europe trade
Alternative shipping route around southern Africa when Suez is disrupted
